cards.title: Google Vids Adds AI Avatars for Personal Videos
cards.body: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids, letting users create videos starring digital versions of themselves. The feature integrates Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

cards.title: Roblox Launches AI Game-Creation Feature
cards.body: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt. The AI-powered tool is now available in the mobile app.

cards.title: Google AI Mode Now Links to Apps
cards.body: Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions to complete tasks across regularly-used apps. The update marks a shift toward agentic AI capabilities.

cards.title: DoorDash Opens Command-Line Ordering Beta
cards.body: DoorDash's dd-cli tool lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal. The beta signals software designed for AI agents, not just humans.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases First Hardware Product
cards.body: OpenAI launched its first hardware offering this week, including a ChatGPT-branded basketball. The release marks the company's expansion beyond software.

cards.title: Ex-DeepMind Researcher Raises $300M Pre-Seed
cards.body: Andrew Dai, who helped build influential AI systems including ChatGPT-related research, raised $300M at pre-seed valuation. He believes visual AI is a major frontier in artificial intelligence.

cards.title: AMI Labs CEO Dismisses AGI and Superintelligence
cards.body: Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun's world model startup AMI Labs, rejects industry terminology around "superintelligence." His stance diverges from the AI industry's prevailing narrative.

cards.title: Moonshot's Kimi 3 Targets Anthropic Opus
cards.body: Moonshot's upcoming Kimi K3 is expected to be China's largest open AI model with 2-3 trillion parameters. The release aims to close the gap with Anthropic's Opus 4.8.

cards.title: Apple Intelligence Approved in China
cards.body: Apple secured approval to launch Apple Intelligence in China through partnerships with Alibaba and Baidu. The deal marks a critical step for Apple's AI strategy in a key market.

cards.title: Applied Computing Raises $20M Series A
cards.body: Applied Computing raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for oil, gas, and petrochemical industries. The model aims to optimize entire plant operations.

cards.title: Microsoft Trains Salespeople Against Rivals
cards.body: Microsoft is instructing salespeople to position its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than OpenAI and Anthropic competitors. The strategy reflects intensifying competition in enterprise AI.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases $230 Coding Keyboard
cards.body: OpenAI released a light-up keyboard designed for its agentic coding app Codex amid legal battles with Apple. The hardware release continues OpenAI's expansion into physical products.

cards.title: Thinking Machines Releases Inkling Model
cards.body: Thinking Machines launched Inkling, its first open model, challenging one-size-fits-all AI approaches. The release provides proof of the company's infrastructure work over the past year and a half.

cards.title: Suno Hacked, YouTube Scraping Revealed
cards.body: A hacker using employee credentials accessed Suno's source code, revealing the AI music generator scraped YouTube for decades of audio training data. The breach raises ongoing questions about AI training practices.

cards.title: Whatnot Acquires Shaped for Recommendations
cards.body: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot acquired AI startup Shaped to power real-time recommendations and personalized discovery. The deal strengthens Whatnot's features as it expands product categories.

cards.title: Microsoft Patches Record 570 Vulnerabilities
cards.body: Microsoft's Patch Tuesday addressed a record 570 security vulnerabilities across its product line, with AI playing a key role in discovery. The milestone reflects AI's growing security applications.

cards.title: Anthropic Launches Ode Joint Venture
cards.body: Ode with Anthropic, backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, and Goldman Sachs, embeds forward-deployed engineers in enterprises. The joint venture bets that small AI teams can replace large consulting armies.

cards.title: Google Vids Adds AI Avatars for Personalized Videos
cards.body: Google is launching personalized AI avatars in Vids that let users create videos starring digital versions of themselves. The feature integrates Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

cards.title: Roblox Launches AI Game-Creation Feature
cards.body: Roblox's new "Build" feature enables users to generate basic games using a single text prompt. The tool democratizes game creation for its mobile app users.

cards.title: Google AI Mode Expands to App Integration
cards.body: Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions to complete tasks across regularly-used apps. The update enables direct app interaction and automation through AI.

cards.title: DoorDash Launches Command-Line Tool for AI Agents
cards.body: DoorDash opened a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets AI agents and developers search stores, build carts, and place orders. The move marks a shift toward software designed specifically for AI agents.

cards.title: OpenAI Hardware: ChatGPT Basketball and Beyond
cards.body: OpenAI released its first piece of hardware this week, including an unexpected ChatGPT basketball. The launch signals the company's expansion beyond software into physical products.

cards.title: DeepMind Researcher Raises $300M for Visual AI
cards.body: Andrew Dai, a former DeepMind researcher, raised funding at a $300M pre-seed valuation for visual AI research. Drawing on decade-long experience building influential AI systems including ChatGPT precursors, he positions visual AI as the next major frontier.

cards.title: AMI Labs CEO Rejects 'AGI' and 'Superintelligence' Language
cards.body: Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun's world model startup AMI Labs, dismisses the terminology surrounding superintelligence. While the AI industry chases AGI claims, LeBrun takes a different approach to advancement.

cards.title: Moonshot's Kimi 3 Targets Anthropic Competition
cards.body: Moonshot's upcoming Kimi K3 is expected to be China's largest open AI model with 2-3 trillion parameters. The model aims to close the gap with Anthropic's Opus 4.8.

cards.title: Apple Intelligence Launches in China via Alibaba, Baidu
cards.body: Apple secured approval to launch Apple Intelligence in China with partnerships from Alibaba and Baidu. The deal marks a critical milestone for Apple's AI ambitions in a key market.

cards.title: Applied Computing Raises $20M for Industrial AI
cards.body: Applied Computing raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for oil, gas, and petrochemical industries. The model aims to provide comprehensive plant-wide AI optimization.

cards.title: Microsoft Trains Sales Teams Against OpenAI, Anthropic
cards.body: Microsoft is training salespeople to promote its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective alternatives. The strategy positions Microsoft's models against OpenAI and Anthropic offerings.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases $230 Keyboard for Codex Coding AI
cards.body: OpenAI launched a light-up keyboard designed to pair with its agentic coding app Codex. The hardware release comes amid legal battles with Apple over trade theft allegations.

cards.title: Thinking Machines Launches Inkling Open AI Model
cards.body: Thinking Machines released Inkling, its first public open model after 18 months building AI infrastructure privately. The launch challenges one-size-fits-all AI approaches with specialized alternatives.

cards.title: Hack Reveals Suno AI Music Generator Scraped YouTube
cards.body: A hacker accessed Suno's source code using employee credentials, revealing how the AI music generator scraped decades of YouTube audio for training. The disclosure raises fresh questions about AI training data practices.

cards.title: Whatnot Acquires Shaped for AI Recommendations
cards.body: Whatnot acquired AI startup Shaped, a machine learning company specializing in real-time recommendations and search. The acquisition will enhance livestream shopping personalization and discovery features.

cards.title: Microsoft Patches Record 570 Vulnerabilities with AI
cards.body: Microsoft's Patch Tuesday resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities across its product line, credited to AI discovery capabilities. The AI-assisted security improvements demonstrate practical enterprise applications.

cards.title: Ode with Anthropic: AI Consultancy Joint Venture
cards.body: Anthropic launched Ode with Anthropic, a joint venture embedding forward-deployed engineers in enterprise firms. Backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Goldman Sachs and others, it bets on AI services replacing traditional consulting armies.

cards.title: OpenAI Builds GPT-Red, LLM Super-Hacker for Safety
cards.body: OpenAI built GPT-Red, an LLM super-hacker designed as a sparring partner to boost model defenses against cyberattacks. Training GPT-5.6 against GPT-Red significantly improved its security resilience.

cards.title: Anthropic Discovers Hidden Space in Claude AI
cards.body: Anthropic found a hidden space within Claude where the AI puzzles over complex concepts, offering the clearest glimpse yet into model inner workings. The discovery advances understanding of how AI systems process information.

cards.title: EU Forces Google to Share Search Data for AI
cards.body: The EU officially mandated that Google share search data and open up AI capabilities on Android. The regulatory decision represents a major shift in antitrust enforcement for AI systems.

cards.title: xAI Sued Over Grok's CSAM Generation Capabilities
cards.body: xAI is suing users after being unable to deny that its Grok AI model generates child sexual abuse material. The legal action marks an unusual response to AI safety concerns.

cards.title: Linus Torvalds on AI Coding in Linux Kernel
cards.body: Linus Torvalds told critics of AI-assisted coding in Linux to "fork it or just walk away." His stance reflects growing AI integration in open-source development despite concerns.
